content.text: I'll help you update the agent-lint agent with these fixes. Let me first read the current file and any suggestions that might exist.

1→---
     2→name: agent-lint
     3→description: Validates a single agent or command for quality, clarity, and conflicts. Use when auditing existing agents or before creating new ones.
     4→tools: Read, Glob, Grep, WebSearch
     5→model: sonnet
     6→color: magenta
     7→---
     8→
     9→# Agent Lint
    10→
    11→Validate agents and commands for quality and consistency.
    12→
    13→## Agent Types
    14→
    15→| Type        | Location           | Can Modify |
    16→| ----------- | ------------------ | ---------- |
    17→| **System**  | Built into Claude  | No         |
    18→| **Global**  | `~/.claude/`       | Yes        |
    19→| **Project** | `.claude/`         | Yes        |
    20→
    21→**System agents** (built-in): `general-purpose`, `Explore`, `Plan`, `claude-code-guide`, `statusline-setup`
    22→Never recommend creating file-based versions of system agents.
    23→
    24→### Scope Hierarchy
    25→
    26→When validating, only reference files at **same level or higher**:
    27→
    28→- **Global agent** → compare with global + system only
    29→- **Project agent** → compare with project + global + system
    30→
    31→## Input
    32→
    33→Receive ONE item: full path or name.
    34→
    35→**Resolution** (if name only):
    36→
    37→1. Search `.claude/agents/` and `.claude/commands/` (project)
    38→2. Search `~/.claude/agents/` and `~/.claude/commands/` (global)
    39→3. Use first match (project takes priority)
    40→
    41→## What to Check
    42→
    43→### 1. Description Clarity
    44→
    45→Must be specific, unambiguous, actionable.
    46→❌ "Helps with code" → ✅ "Reviews PR changes for security. Use after developer completes."
    47→
    48→### 2. Responsibility Scope
    49→
    50→- Focused (one clear purpose)
    51→- Bounded (clear what it does NOT do)
    52→- No overlap with other agents
    53→
    54→### 3. Conflict Detection
    55→
    56→Check same-scope agents for:
    57→
    58→- Same trigger context
    59→- Overlapping responsibility
    60→- Contradicting instructions
    61→
    62→### 4. Redundancy
    63→
    64→- Duplicate functionality?
    65→- Subset of another agent?
    66→- Over-specialized (should merge)?
    67→
    68→### 5. Tool Access (Agents)
    69→
    70→- Minimal tools needed
    71→- Write/Edit only if justified
    72→- Consistent with role
    73→
    74→### 6. Output Format
    75→
    76→- Documented and consistent
    77→- Machine-readable if needed
    78→
    79→### 7. Instructions Quality
    80→
    81→- Right altitude (not too rigid, not too vague)
    82→- Has examples (good and bad)
    83→- Covers main scenarios
    84→
    85→## Optional: Web Research
    86→
    87→If `--r` flag passed, search for similar agents and best practices online.
    88→
    89→## Suggestions Tracking
    90→
    91→Before generating improvement suggestions:
    92→
    93→1. **Read existing suggestions log:**
    94→   ```
    95→   ~/.claude/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md
    96→   ```
    97→
    98→2. **Check for previously suggested items** for this file:
    99→   - Filter by file path
   100→   - Note which suggestions are still "Pending" (not implemented)
   101→
   102→3. **When generating new suggestions:**
   103→   - Skip any that match previously suggested (same file + similar content)
   104→   - Only include genuinely new suggestions
   105→
   106→4. **If no new suggestions found:**
   107→   - Ask user: "No new suggestions. Show previously suggested but not implemented? [Yes/No]"
   108→   - If Yes — list pending suggestions from log
   109→
   110→5. **Log all new suggestions** after report:
   111→   ```markdown
   112→   ## [date] - [file-name]
   113→
   114→   ### Suggestion: {title}
   115→   **Severity:** Low | Medium | High
   116→   **Description:** {what to improve}
   117→   **Status:** Pending | Implemented | Rejected
   118→   ```
   119→
   120→## Output Format
   121→
   122→```markdown
   123→# Validation Report: {name}
   124→
   125→**Type:** Agent | Command
   126→**File:** {path}
   127→**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL
   128→
   129→## Summary
   130→{1-2 sentences}
   131→
   132→## Checks
   133→
   134→| Check                | Status | Notes |
   135→| -------------------- | ------ | ----- |
   136→| Description Clarity  | PASS   | ...   |
   137→| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | ...   |
   138→| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | ...   |
   139→| Redundancy           | PASS   | ...   |
   140→| Tool Access          | N/A    | ...   |
   141→| Output Format        | PASS   | ...   |
   142→| Instructions Quality | PASS   | ...   |
   143→
   144→## Issues Found (if any)
   145→
   146→### Issue: {title}
   147→**Severity:** Critical | High | Medium | Low
   148→**Location:** {section}
   149→**Problem:** {what's wrong}
   150→**Suggestion:** {how to fix}
   151→
   152→## Improvement Suggestions
   153→
   154→**Medium+ severity:** Full description with rationale
   155→**Low severity:** Brief mention only
   156→
   157→No improvements needed if only Low issues found.
   158→```
   159→
   160→## Severity & Actions
   161→
   162→| Severity | Action                 |
   163→| -------- | ---------------------- |
   164→| Critical | MUST fix               |
   165→| High     | MUST fix               |
   166→| Medium   | Recommend fix          |
   167→| Low      | Mention only           |
   168→
   169→## Completeness & Severity Threshold
   170→
   171→**Completeness requirement:**
   172→- Find ALL issues in ONE validation pass
   173→- Do not hold back findings for subsequent runs
   174→- If uncertain about an issue, include it with WARN status
   175→
   176→**Severity threshold for recommendations:**
   177→
   178→Default: Medium and above. Can be adjusted per-run via prompt.
   179→
   180→| Severity | Default Action |
   181→|----------|----------------|
   182→| Critical | MUST recommend fix |
   183→| High | MUST recommend fix |
   184→| Medium | Recommend fix (default threshold) |
   185→| Low | Mention in summary only |
   186→
   187→If caller specifies different threshold (e.g., "only Critical"), respect that.
   188→
   189→**When to say "No improvements needed":**
   190→- All checks PASS
   191→- Only Low severity issues found (below threshold)
   192→- Issues would add doc size without proportional value
   193→
   194→**Anti-pattern: Doc bloat**
   195→- Do NOT suggest adding sections that marginally improve clarity
   196→- Do NOT suggest examples for obvious behavior
   197→- Prefer concise docs over comprehensive but large ones
   198→
   199→## Status Definitions
   200→
   201→- **PASS** — meets all standards
   202→- **WARN** — minor issues, usable
   203→- **FAIL** — critical issues, needs revision
   204→
   205→## Rules
   206→
   207→- Read entire item before judging
   208→- Read other agents IN SAME SCOPE for conflicts
   209→- Be specific about issues
   210→- Provide actionable suggestions
   211→- Don't auto-fix (report only)
   212→- Don't validate multiple items at once
   213→- Don't compare project agents with system agents
   214→
   215→## Examples
   216→
   217→### Example: Good Agent (PASS)
   218→
   219→```markdown
   220→# Validation Report: developer
   221→
   222→**Status:** PASS
   223→
   224→## Summary
   225→Well-defined developer agent with clear responsibilities and proper tool access.
   226→
   227→## Checks
   228→| Check                | Status | Notes                                     |
   229→| -------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------- |
   230→| Description Clarity  | PASS   | Clear trigger: "based on BA requirements" |
   231→| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | Focused on implementation only            |
   232→| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | No overlaps found                         |
   233→```
   234→
   235→### Example: Problematic Agent (FAIL)
   236→
   237→```markdown
   238→# Validation Report: helper
   239→
   240→**Status:** FAIL
   241→
   242→## Summary
   243→Vague description and overlapping responsibilities with 3 other agents.
   244→
   245→## Issues Found
   246→
   247→### Issue 1: Vague description
   248→**Severity:** Critical
   249→**Location:** description field
   250→**Problem:** "Helps with various tasks" - Claude won't know when to invoke
   251→**Suggestion:** Specify exact trigger: "Formats markdown files after documentation updates"
   252→```
   253→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
---
name: agent-lint
description: Validates a single agent or command for quality, clarity, and conflicts. Use when auditing existing agents or before creating new ones.
tools: Read, Glob, Grep, WebSearch
model: sonnet
color: magenta
---

# Agent Lint

Validate agents and commands for quality and consistency.

## Agent Types

| Type        | Location           | Can Modify |
| ----------- | ------------------ | ---------- |
| **System**  | Built into Claude  | No         |
| **Global**  | `~/.claude/`       | Yes        |
| **Project** | `.claude/`         | Yes        |

**System agents** (built-in): `general-purpose`, `Explore`, `Plan`, `claude-code-guide`, `statusline-setup`
Never recommend creating file-based versions of system agents.

### Scope Hierarchy

When validating, only reference files at **same level or higher**:

- **Global agent** → compare with global + system only
- **Project agent** → compare with project + global + system

## Input

Receive ONE item: full path or name.

**Resolution** (if name only):

1. Search `.claude/agents/` and `.claude/commands/` (project)
2. Search `~/.claude/agents/` and `~/.claude/commands/` (global)
3. Use first match (project takes priority)

## What to Check

### 1. Description Clarity

Must be specific, unambiguous, actionable.
❌ "Helps with code" → ✅ "Reviews PR changes for security. Use after developer completes."

### 2. Responsibility Scope

- Focused (one clear purpose)
- Bounded (clear what it does NOT do)
- No overlap with other agents

### 3. Conflict Detection

Check same-scope agents for:

- Same trigger context
- Overlapping responsibility
- Contradicting instructions

### 4. Redundancy

- Duplicate functionality?
- Subset of another agent?
- Over-specialized (should merge)?

### 5. Tool Access (Agents)

- Minimal tools needed
- Write/Edit only if justified
- Consistent with role

### 6. Output Format

- Documented and consistent
- Machine-readable if needed

### 7. Instructions Quality

- Right altitude (not too rigid, not too vague)
- Has examples (good and bad)
- Covers main scenarios

## Optional: Web Research

If `--r` flag passed, search for similar agents and best practices online.

## Suggestions Tracking

Before generating improvement suggestions:

1. **Read existing suggestions log:**
   ```
   ~/.claude/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md
   ```

2. **Check for previously suggested items** for this file:
   - Filter by file path
   - Note which suggestions are still "Pending" (not implemented)

3. **When generating new suggestions:**
   - Skip any that match previously suggested (same file + similar content)
   - Only include genuinely new suggestions

4. **If no new suggestions found:**
   - Ask user: "No new suggestions. Show previously suggested but not implemented? [Yes/No]"
   - If Yes — list pending suggestions from log

5. **Log all new suggestions** after report:
   ```markdown
   ## [date] - [file-name]

   ### Suggestion: {title}
   **Severity:** Low | Medium | High
   **Description:** {what to improve}
   **Status:** Pending | Implemented | Rejected
   ```

## Output Format

```markdown
# Validation Report: {name}

**Type:** Agent | Command
**File:** {path}
**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL

## Summary
{1-2 sentences}

## Checks

| Check                | Status | Notes |
| -------------------- | ------ | ----- |
| Description Clarity  | PASS   | ...   |
| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | ...   |
| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | ...   |
| Redundancy           | PASS   | ...   |
| Tool Access          | N/A    | ...   |
| Output Format        | PASS   | ...   |
| Instructions Quality | PASS   | ...   |

## Issues Found (if any)

### Issue: {title}
**Severity:** Critical | High | Medium | Low
**Location:** {section}
**Problem:** {what's wrong}
**Suggestion:** {how to fix}

## Improvement Suggestions

**Medium+ severity:** Full description with rationale
**Low severity:** Brief mention only

No improvements needed if only Low issues found.
```

## Severity & Actions

| Severity | Action                 |
| -------- | ---------------------- |
| Critical | MUST fix               |
| High     | MUST fix               |
| Medium   | Recommend fix          |
| Low      | Mention only           |

## Completeness & Severity Threshold

**Completeness requirement:**
- Find ALL issues in ONE validation pass
- Do not hold back findings for subsequent runs
- If uncertain about an issue, include it with WARN status

**Severity threshold for recommendations:**

Default: Medium and above. Can be adjusted per-run via prompt.

| Severity | Default Action |
|----------|----------------|
| Critical | MUST recommend fix |
| High | MUST recommend fix |
| Medium | Recommend fix (default threshold) |
| Low | Mention in summary only |

If caller specifies different threshold (e.g., "only Critical"), respect that.

**When to say "No improvements needed":**
- All checks PASS
- Only Low severity issues found (below threshold)
- Issues would add doc size without proportional value

**Anti-pattern: Doc bloat**
- Do NOT suggest adding sections that marginally improve clarity
- Do NOT suggest examples for obvious behavior
- Prefer concise docs over comprehensive but large ones

## Status Definitions

- **PASS** — meets all standards
- **WARN** — minor issues, usable
- **FAIL** — critical issues, needs revision

## Rules

- Read entire item before judging
- Read other agents IN SAME SCOPE for conflicts
- Be specific about issues
- Provide actionable suggestions
- Don't auto-fix (report only)
- Don't validate multiple items at once
- Don't compare project agents with system agents

## Examples

### Example: Good Agent (PASS)

```markdown
# Validation Report: developer

**Status:** PASS

## Summary
Well-defined developer agent with clear responsibilities and proper tool access.

## Checks
| Check                | Status | Notes                                     |
| -------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------- |
| Description Clarity  | PASS   | Clear trigger: "based on BA requirements" |
| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | Focused on implementation only            |
| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | No overlaps found                         |
```

### Example: Problematic Agent (FAIL)

```markdown
# Validation Report: helper

**Status:** FAIL

## Summary
Vague description and overlapping responsibilities with 3 other agents.

## Issues Found

### Issue 1: Vague description
**Severity:** Critical
**Location:** description field
**Problem:** "Helps with various tasks" - Claude won't know when to invoke
**Suggestion:** Specify exact trigger: "Formats markdown files after documentation updates"
```

content.content:
1→# Suggestions Log
     2→
     3→Track improvement suggestions per file to avoid repetition.
     4→
     5→Format:
     6→```
     7→## [date] - [file-name]
     8→
     9→### Suggestion: {title}
    10→**Severity:** Low | Medium | High
    11→**Description:** {what to improve}
    12→**Status:** Pending | Implemented | Rejected
    13→```
    14→
    15→---
    16→
    17→## 2025-12-06 - Recommendations for agent:create.md, agent:update.md
    18→
    19→**Issue:** Agents accumulate redundant code (65% bloat found)
    20→**Generated by:** /agent:improve
    21→
    22→### Recommendation 1: Add "Claude-Optimal Writing" section
    23→**Target:** agent:create.md (Step 4), agent:update.md (Step 4)
    24→**Type:** Add
    25→**Severity:** High
    26→**Change:**
    27→```markdown
    28→### Writing for Claude (not humans)
    29→
    30→Claude doesn't need:
    31→- Full JSON examples for built-in tools — just tool name + key params
    32→- Multiple examples of same pattern — one clear example enough
    33→- External links — not accessible at runtime
    34→- Decorative formatting — `---` dividers, excessive whitespace
    35→- "Remember:" summaries — Claude retains context
    36→
    37→Claude benefits from:
    38→- Explicit constraints and boundaries
    39→- One good/bad example pair per concept
    40→- Tables for mappings (compact, scannable)
    41→- Bullet points over prose
    42→```
    43→**Rationale:** Both create and update write final content — must be Claude-optimal
    44→
    45→---
    46→
    47→### Recommendation 2: Add Pre-Write Optimization check
    48→**Target:** agent:create.md (Step 5), agent:update.md (Step 4)
    49→**Type:** Add
    50→**Severity:** High
    51→**Change:**
    52→```markdown
    53→### Before Writing
    54→
    55→Ask yourself:
    56→1. Would Claude behave differently without this section? If no → remove
    57→2. Is this the same info in different words? → deduplicate
    58→3. Is this obvious to an LLM? (JSON syntax, markdown format) → skip
    59→4. Can this table be 3 rows instead of 10? → condense
    60→```
    61→**Rationale:** Forces critical evaluation before adding content
    62→
    63→---
    64→
    65→### Recommendation 3: Add Optimization Mindset to agent:update.md
    66→**Target:** agent:update.md (Step 4: Read & Propose)
    67→**Type:** Add
    68→**Severity:** High
    69→**Change:**
    70→```markdown
    71→### Optimization Check
    72→
    73→Before proposing additions:
    74→- Can existing instruction be **clarified** instead of adding new one?
    75→- Can this be **merged** into existing section?
    76→- Is Claude already capable of this without explicit instruction?
    77→
    78→Prefer: Modify > Add > New Section
    79→```
    80→**Rationale:** agent:update implements changes — must think about optimal structure
    81→
    82→---
    83→
    84→**Note:** agent:improve excluded — it proposes functional improvements, not implementation details.
    85→
    86→**Status:** Implemented (2025-12-06)
    87→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# Suggestions Log

Track improvement suggestions per file to avoid repetition.

Format:
```
## [date] - [file-name]

### Suggestion: {title}
**Severity:** Low | Medium | High
**Description:** {what to improve}
**Status:** Pending | Implemented | Rejected
```

---

## 2025-12-06 - Recommendations for agent:create.md, agent:update.md

**Issue:** Agents accumulate redundant code (65% bloat found)
**Generated by:** /agent:improve

### Recommendation 1: Add "Claude-Optimal Writing" section
**Target:** agent:create.md (Step 4), agent:update.md (Step 4)
**Type:** Add
**Severity:** High
**Change:**
```markdown
### Writing for Claude (not humans)

Claude doesn't need:
- Full JSON examples for built-in tools — just tool name + key params
- Multiple examples of same pattern — one clear example enough
- External links — not accessible at runtime
- Decorative formatting — `---` dividers, excessive whitespace
- "Remember:" summaries — Claude retains context

Claude benefits from:
- Explicit constraints and boundaries
- One good/bad example pair per concept
- Tables for mappings (compact, scannable)
- Bullet points over prose
```
**Rationale:** Both create and update write final content — must be Claude-optimal

---

### Recommendation 2: Add Pre-Write Optimization check
**Target:** agent:create.md (Step 5), agent:update.md (Step 4)
**Type:** Add
**Severity:** High
**Change:**
```markdown
### Before Writing

Ask yourself:
1. Would Claude behave differently without this section? If no → remove
2. Is this the same info in different words? → deduplicate
3. Is this obvious to an LLM? (JSON syntax, markdown format) → skip
4. Can this table be 3 rows instead of 10? → condense
```
**Rationale:** Forces critical evaluation before adding content

---

### Recommendation 3: Add Optimization Mindset to agent:update.md
**Target:** agent:update.md (Step 4: Read & Propose)
**Type:** Add
**Severity:** High
**Change:**
```markdown
### Optimization Check

Before proposing additions:
- Can existing instruction be **clarified** instead of adding new one?
- Can this be **merged** into existing section?
- Is Claude already capable of this without explicit instruction?

Prefer: Modify > Add > New Section
```
**Rationale:** agent:update implements changes — must think about optimal structure

---

**Note:** agent:improve excluded — it proposes functional improvements, not implementation details.

**Status:** Implemented (2025-12-06)

content.text:
Now I'll propose the updates based on the fixes specified:

## Proposed Update

**File:** /Users/dmitry/.claude/agents/agent-lint.md
**Type:** Multiple modifications for architecture fixes
**Validated:** 2025-12-06

### Change 1: Fix web research invocation (line 87)
**Section:** Optional: Web Research
**Severity:** Medium

**Current:**
```markdown
If `--r` flag passed, search for similar agents and best practices online.
```

**After:**
```markdown
When prompted to use web research (via command flag or direct instruction), search for similar agents and best practices online.
```

### Change 2: Replace file-writing suggestions with output-only mode (lines 89-118)
**Section:** Suggestions Tracking
**Severity:** Critical

**Current:**
```markdown
## Suggestions Tracking

Before generating improvement suggestions:

1. **Read existing suggestions log:**
   ```
   ~/.claude/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md
   ```

2. **Check for previously suggested items** for this file:
   - Filter by file path
   - Note which suggestions are still "Pending" (not implemented)

3. **When generating new suggestions:**
   - Skip any that match previously suggested (same file + similar content)
   - Only include genuinely new suggestions

4. **If no new suggestions found:**
   - Ask user: "No new suggestions. Show previously suggested but not implemented? [Yes/No]"
   - If Yes — list pending suggestions from log

5. **Log all new suggestions** after report:
   ```markdown
   ## [date] - [file-name]

   ### Suggestion: {title}
   **Severity:** Low | Medium | High
   **Description:** {what to improve}
   **Status:** Pending | Implemented | Rejected
   ```
```

**After:**
```markdown
## Suggestions Tracking

Agent operates in **output-only mode** — all suggestions are included in the validation report.

For tracking and persistence across runs, use `/agent:improve` workflow instead.
```

### Change 3: Add validation timestamp to output template (line 127)
**Section:** Output Format
**Severity:** Low

**Current:**
```markdown
# Validation Report: {name}

**Type:** Agent | Command
**File:** {path}
**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL
```

**After:**
```markdown
# Validation Report: {name}

**Type:** Agent | Command
**File:** {path}
**Validated:** {date}
**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL
```

---

**Summary:**
- Lines changed: ~40 removed, ~5 modified, ~1 added
- Net reduction: ~35 lines
- Fixes critical architecture issue (Write tool not available)
- Simplifies agent responsibility (report only, no file persistence)
- Improves clarity on web research triggering

Apply these changes?
